api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/_.purpose: Low-level serialization/deserialization utilities for the generated Python SDK: SdkJSONEncoder, the MutableMapping-based _Model base (model_base.py), format-aware datetime/bytes/timedelta/decimal converters, and the legacy Autorest-style Model/Serializer/Deserializer (serialization.py). Fully generated — never hand-edited.

patterns.name: Format-dispatch deserialization via get_deserializer()
patterns.description: Route datetime/bytes/timedelta/decimal deserialization through get_deserializer(annotation, rf); it selects from _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING (keyed by Python type) or _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING_WITHFORMAT (keyed by wire format e.g. 'rfc7231', 'base64url'). Never hand-roll datetime/bytes parsing.

patterns.name: SdkJSONEncoder with exclude_readonly for request bodies
patterns.description: Build request bodies via json.dumps(payload, cls=SdkJSONEncoder, exclude_readonly=True). default() strips fields whose rest_field _visibility == ['read'] and handles datetime, bytes, timedelta, decimal, _Null, and _Model instances.

patterns.name: Two distinct Model base classes — do not mix
patterns.description: model_base.py defines _Model (MutableMapping-backed, rest_field annotations); serialization.py defines Model (_attribute_map class var, Autorest Serializer/Deserializer). New dataclass-style models extend _Model; _operations.py uses serialization.Model via _SERIALIZER. Never subclass _Model and also declare _attribute_map.

patterns.name: _MyMutableMapping cache invalidation on __setitem__
patterns.description: _MyMutableMapping clears the _deserialized_<key> attribute cache on every __setitem__. Mutating _data directly bypasses invalidation; always go through attribute or dict access.

key_file_guides.role: Defines _Model, _MyMutableMapping, rest_field, rest_discriminator, SdkJSONEncoder, and all _deserialize_*/_serialize_* helpers plus get_deserializer(). Core of the new-codegen path.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mutating _data directly bypasses the _deserialized_<key> cache invalidation in __setitem__; always go through attribute or dict interface.

key_file_guides.role: Legacy Autorest Model base, Serializer, Deserializer, RawDeserializer, key transformers (attribute_transformer, full_restapi_key_transformer). Consumed by _operations.py via the module-level _SERIALIZER singleton.
key_file_guides.watch_for: _SERIALIZER has client_side_validation=False; do not create per-call Serializer instances or re-enable validation.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports SdkJSONEncoder, Model, rest_field, rest_discriminator as the package public surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new public symbol must also appear in __all__.

anti_patterns: Hand-rolling datetime/bytes serialization instead of using _serialize_datetime / _serialize_bytes / get_deserializer()
Editing model_base.py or serialization.py — they are generated and overwritten by make gen-api
Creating per-request Serializer() instances — _SERIALIZER is a module-level singleton with client_side_validation=False
Subclassing _Model and also declaring _attribute_map — mutually exclusive codegen paths
Importing corehttp internals not already imported here (fragile transitive dependency)

decisions.decision: Two Model base classes coexist: _Model (MutableMapping, rest_field) and serialization.Model (attribute_map, Autorest)
decisions.rationale: SDK was generated with newer codegen (_Model) but retains the legacy Autorest Model for backwards compatibility and XML support.

decisions.decision: _DESERIALIZE_MAPPING_WITHFORMAT separates format-aware converters from type-based converters
decisions.rationale: The same Python type (datetime) maps to multiple wire formats (rfc3339, rfc7231, unix-timestamp); splitting lets get_deserializer() check format first, then fall back to type-only dispatch.

code_examples.scenario: Deserialize a datetime field with format awareness from a rest_field annotation
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated._utils.model_base import get_deserializer
deserializer = get_deserializer(datetime, rf)  # rf._format may be 'rfc7231', 'unix-timestamp', or None
value = deserializer(raw_value)  # returns a timezone-aware datetime

api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/a.purpose: Async (asyncio) mirror of the sync operations layer. Each Operations class wraps a resource group and exposes `async def` methods that execute HTTP via AsyncPipelineClient, delegating all request-building to the sync build_* functions in the parent operations/_operations.py.

patterns.name: Delegate request construction to sync build_* functions
patterns.description: Every async method imports build_<resource>_<action>_request from ...operations._operations and never duplicates URL/query-param construction inline.

patterns.name: Positional-first constructor injection
patterns.description: Each Operations __init__ pops (client, config, serializer, deserializer) from positional args first, then kwargs — never named-only parameters.
patterns.example: self._client: AsyncPipelineClient = input_args.pop(0) if input_args else kwargs.pop('client')

patterns.name: Per-method error_map with typed Problem deserialization
patterns.description: Each method seeds a MutableMapping error_map with 404/409/304 defaults, then deserializes typed Problem models (BadRequestProblemResponse, etc.) per status code before raising HttpResponseError.

patterns.name: Stream-safe response cleanup on error
patterns.description: On non-2xx when _stream=True, call `await response.read()` inside a try/except for StreamConsumedError/StreamClosedError before map_error to prevent socket leaks.

patterns.name: _patch.py customization hook
patterns.description: _patch.py is the only sanctioned place to add customizations; __init__.py imports from ._patch and calls patch_sdk() at load. Keep it minimal.

key_file_guides.role: Every async Operations class and its async methods. Generated — accessed through OpenMeterClient attributes, not instantiated directly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do NOT instantiate Operations classes directly. Adding methods here without a corresponding sync build_* function in the parent operations package breaks the reuse pattern.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all Operations classes and applies _patch overrides via _patch_sdk(). Mirrors the sync operations __init__.py.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new Operations class in _operations.py must be added to both __all__ and the import list here, and mirrored in the sync __init__.py.

key_file_guides.role: Empty stub for SDK customizations; imported/executed by __init__.py. Stable seam preserved across regenerations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add business logic; its sole purpose is to survive code regeneration.

anti_patterns: Duplicating URL/query-param construction inside async methods instead of calling shared sync build_* functions
Instantiating Operations classes directly rather than via OpenMeterClient attributes
Adding error handling or deserialization outside the per-method error_map block
Editing _operations.py as if hand-written — it is generated and overwritten on next SDK generation
Raising before `await response.read()` on a streaming error response (leaks the socket)

decisions.decision: Async operations delegate all request construction to sync build_* functions
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating URL/parameter logic across sync/async; one change to a request builder propagates to both execution paths.

decisions.decision: Per-method typed error deserialization instead of a shared handler
decisions.rationale: Different endpoints return different Problem subtypes; inline deserialization keeps error context close to the operation contract.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new async operation method following the existing pattern
code_examples.code:
from ...operations._operations import build_my_resource_list_request
from ..._utils.model_base import _deserialize, _failsafe_deserialize
from ... import models as _models

async def list(self, *, page: Optional[int] = None, **kwargs: Any) -> _models.MyResourcePaginatedResponse:
    error_map: MutableMapping = {404: ResourceNotFoundError, 409: ResourceExistsError, 304: ResourceNotModifiedError}
    error_map.update(kwargs.pop('error_map', {}) or {})
    _request = build_my_resource_list_request(page=page, headers=kwargs.pop('headers', {}) or {}, params=kwargs.pop('params', {}) or {})
    _request.url = self._client.format_url(_request.url, **path_format_arguments)

api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/m.purpose: Auto-generated Python model classes (~200+ in _models.py) and string enums (_enums.py) for the entire OpenMeter API surface — the single source of truth for Python-typed API objects in the SDK. Only _patch.py is safe to modify.

patterns.name: rest_field declaration with visibility and name mapping
patterns.description: Each field is a class variable via rest_field(name='camelCaseName', visibility=[...], format='rfc3339') mapping snake_case to JSON camelCase. Required fields have no default; optional are Optional[T] = rest_field(...). visibility controls which CRUD operations include the field.

patterns.name: Dual @overload __init__
patterns.description: Every model has two @overload signatures: keyword args (IDE completion of required fields) and mapping: Mapping[str, Any] (raw-dict construction from parsed JSON). The real __init__ calls super().__init__(*args, **kwargs).

patterns.name: CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta for all string enums
patterns.description: Every enum in _enums.py inherits (str, Enum) with metaclass=CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta from corehttp.utils, enabling case-insensitive lookup so wire comparisons work regardless of server casing.

patterns.name: _patch.py as the only safe customization point
patterns.description: _patch.py is preserved across regenerations. Add helpers/monkey-patches/public symbols here; new symbols must be listed in _patch.__all__ to appear in models.__all__. patch_sdk() must remain callable.

key_file_guides.role: All ~200+ generated dataclass-style model classes extending _Model. Authoritative definition of every API request/response shape.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated by make gen-api. All customizations go in _patch.py.

key_file_guides.role: All string enums used across models and operations; each uses CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta for wire-value matching.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. Never add a plain str Enum without CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all models/enums and applies _patch via _patch_sdk(); __all__ is the authoritative public API of this subpackage.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerated. New symbols added in _patch.py must appear in _patch.__all__ to propagate here.

key_file_guides.role: The only hand-editable file; place customizations that must survive regeneration here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain callable even if empty; never remove it.

anti_patterns: Editing _models.py or _enums.py directly — overwritten by make gen-api
Subclassing generated models outside _patch.py — breaks regeneration idempotency
Using a plain str Enum without CaseInsensitiveEnumMeta — breaks case-insensitive wire matching
Adding public symbols to _patch.py without listing them in _patch.__all__ — they won't reach models.__all__
Constructing models with positional arguments — use keyword args or a Mapping dict

decisions.decision: All models are MutableMapping-backed (_Model) rather than plain dataclasses
decisions.rationale: Enables dict-style access and JSON serialization without a separate to_dict() step, matching the corehttp pipeline and SdkJSONEncoder's iteration over items().

decisions.decision: _patch.py is the sole customization point preserved across regenerations
decisions.rationale: Generated code stays pristine for automated regeneration; _patch.py is a stable escape hatch that survives make gen-api without merge conflicts.

code_examples.scenario: Construct a model using typed kwargs or a raw dict from parsed JSON
code_examples.code:
from openmeter._generated.models import Customer, AppType
customer = Customer(name='Acme', external_mapping_key='acme-001')
customer = Customer({'name': 'Acme', 'externalMappingKey': 'acme-001'})
app_type = AppType('STRIPE')  # == AppType.STRIPE
